entries.description: Started Session 1. session-state.json flipped currentSession null -> 1, status not-started -> in-progress, lifecycleState null -> work_in_progress. work_started event appended to session-events.jsonl. Budget for Set 026 declared at $10.00; verification path for this set will be manual-via-other-engine (Session 1 itself deletes the daemon path that supports outsource-last verification).

Deleted 10 Python source modules covering the outsource-last / verifier-daemon path: ai_router/queue_status.py, ai_router/heartbeat_status.py, ai_router/queue_db.py, ai_router/queue_verification.py, ai_router/daemon_pid.py, ai_router/orchestrator_role.py, ai_router/restart_role.py, ai_router/role_status.py, ai_router/capacity.py, ai_router/verifier_role.py.

Deleted 17 Python test files for the removed modules: test_capacity.py, test_capacity_signal_wiring.py, test_close_session_session4.py, test_close_session_verification_wait.py, test_cost_report_outsource_last.py, test_daemon_pid.py, test_failure_injection.py, test_heartbeat_status.py, test_mode_aware_route.py, test_orchestrator_role.py, test_queue_db.py, test_queue_followups.py, test_queue_status.py, test_restart_role.py, test_role_status.py, test_verifier_role.py, test_verifier_run_verification.py. Also deleted test_close_out.py (entirely mode-aware) and test_close_out_e2e.py (entirely mode-aware end-to-end). modeBadge.test.ts in the extension test suite also deleted.
Deleted ai_router/docs/two-cli-workflow.md â€” operating guide for outsource-last mode is no longer applicable.

Heavy Python source edits: ai_router/__init__.py (removed all queue_db/daemon_pid/verifier_role/orchestrator_role imports + exports, ModeConfig/OUTSOURCE_MODES/DEFAULT_OUTSOURCE_MODE/parse_mode_config/read_mode_config/validate_mode_config/ROLE_VALUES exports, _resolve_outsource_mode + _enqueue_route_message, route()'s outsource-last branch, verify()'s pending short-circuit, _FORCE_SYNC_TASK_TYPES, pending/message_id/queue_provider fields from RouteResult and VerificationResult); ai_router/close_session.py (removed queue_db/queue_verification imports, --timeout flag and validation, _wait_for_verifications call + per-message ledger events + timeout/failed branches, verification_timeout exit code, queue-citation repair case, queue_base_dir/poll_interval/sleep/monotonic params); ai_router/close_out.py (rewrote single-path implementation, removed mode-aware branching and close_session_runner injection); ai_router/session_state.py (removed ModeConfig, OUTSOURCE_MODES, ROLE_VALUES, DEFAULT_OUTSOURCE_MODE, parse_mode_config, read_mode_config, validate_mode_config; kept _extract_session_set_configuration_block for _read_total_sessions_from_spec); ai_router/disposition.py (VERIFICATION_METHODS changed from ('api','queue') to ('api','manual','skipped'); removed queue-specific validation rules); ai_router/cost_report.py (removed capacity import, _build_subscription_utilization, _print_outsource_last_report, outsource_mode/subscription_utilization keys, mode-aware branch); ai_router/reconciler.py (removed rerun_verification_timeout action and verification-timeout result mapping; removed timeout=1 namespace field); ai_router/router-config.yaml (narrative comments about outsource-last under session-close-out task type and task_type_params updated); ai_router/schemas/disposition.schema.json (verification_method enum updated from ['api','queue'] to ['api','manual','skipped']; queue conditional clause removed); ai_router/__init__.py __version__ bumped 0.2.5 -> 0.3.0.

Cleaned up tests that depended on removed surface: deleted TestParseModeConfig/TestReadModeConfig/TestValidateModeConfig/TestConstants(outsource bits) from test_session_state_v2.py via Python script; renamed _valid_disposition_completed_queue to _valid_disposition_completed_manual + removed test_queue_method_requires_message_ids + test_schema_rejects_queue_without_message_ids in test_disposition.py; updated test_verification_methods_match_spec to expect {'api','manual','skipped'}; removed --timeout argparse cases from test_close_session_skeleton.py + verification_timeout from RESULT_TO_EXIT_CODE table; removed verification_timeout/rerun_verification_timeout parameter from test_reconciler.py. Final pytest: 385 passed, 0 failed.

TypeScript extension edits: tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/types.ts (removed OutsourceMode type + outsourceMode field on SessionSetConfig); tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/fileSystem.ts (parseSessionSetConfig no longer reads outsourceMode); tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/providers/SessionSetsProvider.ts (removed Mode tooltip line and rewrote modeBadge as a no-op stub); tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/wizard/sessionGenPrompt.ts (removed outsourceMode line from the generated spec config block); stripped outsourceMode literals from cancelTreeView.test.ts, forceClosedBadge.test.ts, sessionSetsProvider.test.ts via Python script. npx tsc --outDir out compiles clean (exit 0).
Bumped tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/package.json + package-lock.json from 0.13.14 to 0.13.15. ai_router/__init__.py __version__ bumped to 0.3.0 (already noted under strip-mode-aware-branches).

Created ai_router/CHANGELOG.md with [0.3.0] entry documenting the breaking changes (deleted modules, removed public symbols, removed route() parameters, simplified close_session/close_out/cost_report). Updated tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/CHANGELOG.md with [0.13.15] entry citing v0.13.14's 'CLIs stay' promise and the reversal, justified by Marketplace download count of 3 (all operator's own). Both CHANGELOG entries note that Session 1 is PARTIAL: docs scrubs and 26 historical spec.md scrubs are deferred to a follow-up session. Added 'ai_router/local-overrides.yaml' to .gitignore preemptively for Session 2's file creation.
Final pytest + npx tsc gates run: pytest 385 passed (0 failed) across the surviving test suite; npx tsc --outDir out exits 0 (clean compile). No new build/test regressions introduced.

PARTIAL STOP â€” Session 1 not closed. Deferred to a follow-up session (operator decision required): (a) doc scrubs in docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md, docs/adoption-bootstrap.md, docs/planning/session-set-authoring-guide.md, ai_router/docs/close-out.md, and docs/spec-md-schema.md (still mention outsourceMode / verifier daemon / subscription CLI as live concepts); (b) scrub literal 'outsourceMode: <value>' YAML config line from 26 historical docs/session-sets/*/spec.md files; (c) end-of-session cross-provider verification (manual via IDE-agent paste path per the $10 budget tier). Acceptance criterion 'git grep -i outsourcemode|queue_db|verifier daemon|subscription cli returns zero hits' is NOT satisfied yet â€” narrative references remain in docs and historical specs. The CODE surface, however, is clean of the removed symbols (Python imports, exports, types; TS types and parser). Session is left in-progress; close_session has NOT been invoked. Operator to decide whether to (1) commit this partial state and continue Session 1b in a fresh conversation; (2) push through the docs+scrubs in this same conversation if budget allows; (3) split Session 1 retroactively into 1a + 1b in the spec and treat this as Session 1a complete.
[1a] Deleted ~10 Python modules, mode-aware branches, test files; bumped versions to ai_router 0.3.0 + extension 0.13.15. (Completed in commit dda0a5f.)
[1a] Deleted ai_router/docs/two-cli-workflow.md.

[1b] Removed all outsourceMode references from 7 active docs (ai-led-session-workflow, adoption-bootstrap, close-out.md, spec-md-schema, disposition-schema, quick-start, CLAUDE/AGENTS/GEMINI.md) and 2 Python source files. Rebuilt dist/extension.js, deleted orphaned modeBadge test artifacts.
[1b] Removed outsourceMode: config line from all 25 historical docs/session-sets/*/spec.md files.
pytest 385/385 green; tsc clean. Committed and pushed (3a0defa).

Edited docs/adoption-bootstrap.md Step 5: replaced three separate non-zero-tier dialog boxes (less-than-$20, $20-$99, $100+) with a single NTE ask backed by empirical range data ($0.05-$0.80/call; 3-session set $0.15-$2.50). Kept $0 zero-budget path (manual/skipped) unchanged. Added explicit tier-to-mode mapping table to the mode field description in the field reference. Added verification_nte_usd to the YAML example and field reference. Fixed stale references: 'outsourcing budget' comment -> 'verification budget', 'an outsource mode' -> 'a verification budget' in End of bootstrap, Step 9 More info pointer updated from 'four-tier mapping' to 'two-tier (zero vs. non-zero) budget model'.

Edited docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md Cost-budgeted verification modes section: collapsed four-row budget tier table (zero-budget/limited-budget/middle-tier/ample-budget) to two-row version (zero-budget / non-zero budget); removed 50%-of-threshold tier-upgrade prompt reference; updated Rule 2 Interaction paragraph ('limited / middle / ample tiers' -> 'non-zero budget'); added verification_nte_usd bullet to 'What this means at session execution time' subsection; changed 'outsourcing/API budget threshold' to 'API-verification budget threshold'.

Created ai_router/budget.yaml for this repo: threshold_usd=10, threshold_scope=project-lifetime, mode=limited-budget, verification_method=api, verification_nte_usd=10. First explicit budget for this repo. .gitignore already had local-overrides.yaml from Session 1 (step 4 of spec is no-op).

Cross-provider verification via gpt-5-4. Round 1: ISSUES_FOUND (Minor) - missing explicit tier-to-mode mapping table and stale 'see Step 5 boundaries' reference. Fixed. Round 2: ISSUES_FOUND (Minor) - stale 'outsourcing budget' YAML comment and 'outsource mode' in End of bootstrap. Fixed. Round 3: ISSUES_FOUND (Minor) - stale 'outsourcing/API' in workflow doc intro. Fixed. Round 4: VERIFIED. Total verification cost: $0.38 (cumulative NTE: $0.38 of $10.00).

Added display_label and enabled fields to all three providers (anthropic/google/openai) in ai_router/router-config.yaml. Added routing.outsourcing_mode: whenever-helpful to the routing block. These fields are Set 025 Appendix B schema additions for the webview editor.

Created ai_router/secret_resolver.py exporting resolve_secret(name, source='env') -> str | None and register_backend(). Env-var backend registered as 'env' (only backend in Set 026). Empty-string normalized to None. Registry pattern allows future backends (secretStorage, keyring) without touching callers. Exported from ai_router package public surface alongside register_backend. Added try/except import pattern in config.py and providers.py for package vs. test context compatibility.

Edited ai_router/config.py: (1) apply Set-026 field defaults on load (display_label, enabled, routing.outsourcing_mode) so old configs without these fields still load cleanly; (2) validate models.*.provider against the providers: block, raising clear error on dangling references; (3) read ai_router/local-overrides.yaml if present and merge per Appendix B precedence (local > shared > default). Allowlist enforced: routing.outsourcing_mode, providers.display_label, providers.enabled, notifications, decision_review. New providers/models in local-overrides raise ValueError. Unknown keys warn-and-ignore. API key validation now uses resolve_secret() instead of os.environ.get(). Edited ai_router/providers.py: all three provider callers (_call_anthropic, _call_google, _call_openai) now use resolve_secret() instead of os.environ[].

Created ai_router/migrate_router_config.py: idempotent forward migration using ruamel.yaml AST round-trip for comment preservation. Injects display_label (title-cased key name) and enabled: true per provider if absent; injects routing.outsourcing_mode: whenever-helpful if absent. Renames threshold_scope -> scope per Appendix B rules (project-lifetime -> per-project, per-session-set preserved, monthly -> per-project + period: monthly with deprecation warning). Injects warn_at_percent: 80 if absent. Exit codes: 0 = success/no-op, 1 = parse error. Run with python -m ai_router.migrate_router_config. Added ruamel.yaml>=0.18 to requirements.txt. Ran migration against this repo's router-config.yaml (no changes needed) and budget.yaml (threshold_scope -> scope: per-project, warn_at_percent: 80 added). Second run confirmed idempotent (no changes).

Local-overrides merge logic implemented in config.py._apply_local_overrides. Covered by test_local_overrides_merge.py: 9 tests verifying precedence (local wins for routing/provider/enabled), local-only sections (notifications/decision_review merged into config), allowlist violations (ValueError for routing.tier1_max_complexity and providers.anthropic.api_key_env), new-provider rejection, and unknown-key warning-and-ignore behavior.

Created 5 new test files: test_secret_resolver_env_backend.py (6 tests: present/absent/empty/case-sensitivity/unknown-backend/custom-backend; case-sensitivity test skipped on Windows since os.environ is case-insensitive there), test_migrate_router_config_idempotent.py (6 tests: idempotency, display_label/enabled/outsourcing_mode injection, missing-files no-raise), test_migrate_router_config_threshold_scope_rules.py (6 tests: all four threshold_scope mappings + warn_at_percent), test_migrate_router_config_preserves_comments.py (3 tests: router comments, budget comments, post-migration YAML validity), test_local_overrides_merge.py (9 tests). Final pytest: 414 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed.

Cross-provider verification via gpt-5-4 (session-verification task type). Verdict: Accept / VERIFIED. No blocking issues found. Findings: resolve_secret design correct; migration idempotency sound; allowlist correct; env-var exclusions correct. Total verification spend: ~$0.162 (NTE cumulative: ~$0.54 of $10.00).

Added yaml@^2.7.0 (Eemeli Aro AST-mode round-trip) and ajv@^8.17.1 to tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/package.json dependencies. npm install completed. Registered dabbler.openConfigEditor command in package.json with icon $(settings-gear). Wired registerConfigEditorCommand in src/extension.ts via the existing safeRegister pattern.

Created src/configEditor/yamlReadWrite.ts: readYamlFile (returns null when absent, throws on parse error), writeYamlFile (tmp+rename atomic write, cleans up .tmp on error), parseDocumentFromText (AST Document via yaml.parseDocument). Comment-preserving round-trip relies on yaml@2.x Document.toString() semantics.

Created src/configEditor/schemaValidator.ts: three ajv-compiled schemas (router-config, budget, local-overrides). validateBatch() runs all three + cross-file invariants: env-var name shape (^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$), model-provider dangling reference, local-overrides allowlist (rejects verification_method + scope as project-canonical), local-only-provider rejection. ValidationError carries { file, path, message }.

Created src/configEditor/ConfigEditorPanel.ts: singleton panel (createOrShow reveals existing), findAiRouterDir walks workspace folders, loadFiles reads all 3 YAML files via readYamlFile, validateBatch on load, drift-banner when invalid, 6-placeholder section nav (chevron buttons + content areas), save-time round-trip write (tmp+rename via writeYamlFile), graceful no-workspace and missing-router-config HTML. registerConfigEditorCommand exported for extension.ts.

Created 3 test files: yamlReadWrite.test.ts (9 tests: null-on-missing, valid-read, comment-preservation, multi-line, unicode, atomic-write, round-trip, parse-error, parse-correct); schemaValidator.test.ts (20 tests covering all Appendix B validation rules: required fields, threshold bounds, warn_at_percent bounds, verification_method enum, scope enum, provider ref resolve, env-var name shape, allowlist denials, local-only-provider rejection, allowed overrides); configEditor-foundation.test.ts (save-time atomicity x3, load-time validation x3, panel lifecycle x1 in VS Code electron context). tsc clean; 29 unit-testable tests pass via mocha/ts-node.

Load flow wired in ConfigEditorPanel._loadFiles: walks workspace folders for ai_router/, reads router-config.yaml + budget.yaml + local-overrides.yaml (optional) via readYamlFile, surfaces YAML parse errors per-file via _parseIssues, runs validateBatch on cleanly-parsed files only, and renders a 'Drift detected' or 'YAML parse error' banner above the section nav when either fails. Missing-file fallback now triggers for both router-config.yaml and budget.yaml (per Set 025 spec, both are project-canonical).

Save flow wired in ConfigEditorPanel._handleSave: aborts if parse errors are present; otherwise re-runs validateBatch on the in-memory Documents, aborts on validation failure with an inline error message, then writes each Document atomically via writeYamlFile (tmp + rename per Set 025 Appendix B). Saves all three files in sequence; half-batch-recovery dialog ships in Session 5 (Session 4 only records lastSavedAt; content-hash drift detection deferred). Validation paths are HTML-escaped in the drift banner to prevent injection via YAML keys.

Cross-provider verification via gpt-5-4 (session-verification task type). Five rounds total. Round 1: 4 Major (empty api_key_env bypass; local-overrides api_key_env not validated; additionalProperties: false missing; YAML parse errors not surfaced). Round 2: 3 Major (residual schema closure gaps; models block in local-overrides not rejected; missing budget.yaml silently accepted). Round 3: 1 Major (XSS-style injection in drift banner) + 1 Minor (missing-file message hardcoded to router-config.yaml). Round 4: 1 Major (router-config/budget schema-strictness — declined; documented in code as deliberate scope decision since router-config carries many non-Appendix-B fields) + 2 Minor (duplicate errors between Ajv and custom checker; inconsistent ai_router/ prefix in file list). Round 5: 1 Minor (additionalProperties errors lose offending key path — fixed to use _ajvErrorPath). All Blocker/Major issues resolved or documented as out-of-scope. Final verdict: ACCEPT-WITH-FIXES. Total verification spend: $1.31 across 5 rounds (cumulative Set 026: $1.85 of $10.00 NTE). Files patched in response: schemaValidator.ts (empty-string env-var fix; local-overrides additionalProperties: false; pushover env-var validation; display_label denial; deduped overlapping errors; _ajvErrorPath helper), yamlReadWrite.ts (collectParseErrors; ParsedYaml.parseErrors), ConfigEditorPanel.ts (parse-error banner; drift-banner HTML escaping; required budget.yaml; consistent ai_router/ prefix; missing-file rendering uses path.basename; fixed `as HTMLElement` cast in webview script that would have thrown SyntaxError). Test count: 29 → 38 unit tests passing.

Created 6 section render modules at tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/configEditor/sections/: routingAndVerificationSection.ts (two decoupled dropdowns; outsourcing-Disabled greys out 'Automatic via API'; manual-template URL surfaces conditionally), budgetSection.ts (threshold input + scope dropdown + warn-at-percent range + 3-state preview re-rendered live by client-side JS; cost-messaging copy with explicit dollar range, multi-week scale ($0-$200/month, $5-$50 per 2-3 week set), open-source caveat, dashboard pointer per feedback_user_facing_cost_messaging memory), providersTableSection.ts (variable-length table; per-row enabled checkbox + display label + read-only ID + env-var name + ✓/(unset) presence badge + base_url + popover toggle), significanceFlaggingSection.ts (read-only doc; Run-command-now button + honor-annotations toggle with default ON), notificationsSection.ts (Pushover enable toggle + two env-var-name inputs + disabled test-notification button labelled 'wired in Set 026 Session 7'), localOverridesSummarySection.ts (walks the loaded local-overrides object emitting one row per leaf path; shared/local side-by-side; absent/empty/populated states distinct). Plus shared types.ts (SectionState interface) and helpers.ts (escapeHtml, getByPath, fieldSource computing local>shared>default precedence, indicatorHtml, envVarBadge). Each section exports render(state)=>{ html } and is a pure function — no vscode imports.

Implemented half-batch recovery state machine in ConfigEditorPanel.ts + patch.ts. (a) New patch.ts module exports applyPatch(routerDoc, budgetDoc, localOverridesDoc, SavePayload) -> PatchApplyResult with per-field write-on-change semantics (setIfChanged helper compares getIn(path) === value before mutating, avoiding no-op rewrites); routes each Appendix-B field to its canonical YAML; promotes/demotes between shared (router-config/budget) and local (local-overrides) on the operator's source toggle, including delete-from-other-side cleanup. Includes pruneEmptyProvidersBlock + emptyLocalOverridesDoc + stringContentHash + docContentHash helpers. (b) ConfigEditorPanel._handleSave clones each loaded Document via parseDocumentFromText(doc.toString()) before patching, validates the clones, and only commits clones to _loaded.*Doc after validation passes — a failed validation leaves disk + in-memory state untouched. (c) Save flow pre-computes pending bytes per file, attempts writes with atomic tmp+rename, and on partial failure stores succeeded/failed/pendingContents in _recovery so _retryFailedWrite persists the exact cached bytes (not whatever _loaded re-read from disk via _refresh). (d) _detectDrift preserves an active failed-write recovery (does not overwrite). Drift comparison uses raw text (not hashes) so truncation-to-empty registers as drift; strict !== null checks. (e) _captureSnapshot uses doc.toString() for files that were written and raw loaded text for files untouched; for unchanged local-overrides files, baselines against the on-disk text so subsequent loads don't false-positive on drift. (f) _reapplyLastSave writes _lastSaveSnapshot bytes via the same per-file recovery pattern; handles file-deletion when snapshot.localOverridesText === null. (g) Banner buttons (Retry / Accept-as-baseline / Re-apply) wired through postMessage.

(shared)/(local override)/(default) indicator system. helpers.fieldSource(state, sharedObject, sharedPath, localPath, allowed) computes the source from the loaded state per Appendix B's precedence rules (local > shared > default; not-overridable fields suppress the indicator entirely). helpers.indicatorHtml renders the badge with data-source + data-field attributes; client-side JS toggles between shared <-> local on click (skips default/not-overridable). On Save, the gather function reads the indicator's current data-source attribute and posts it as part of the OverridableValue<T>.source field. The host's applyPatch then routes the field to the correct file and deletes from the other side. Indicator visibility matches Appendix B: verification_method, scope, and display_label show no indicator (not locally overridable); routing.outsourcing_mode, threshold_usd, warn_at_percent, providers.*.enabled/api_key_env/base_url, honor_annotations, and notifications.pushover.* all show appropriate indicators.

Created 6 section test files + 2 supporting test files in src/test/suite/: routingAndVerificationSection.test.ts (6 tests: dropdown selection from shared, disabled-outsourcing greys api, manual-template conditional, local override indicator, shared indicator, not-overridable suppression), budgetSection.test.ts (6 tests: all-four-cost-messaging-elements assertion enforcing multi-week scale, 3-state preview math, local threshold override, scope no-indicator, per-session option visibility), providersTableSection.test.ts (8 tests: row-per-provider, ✓/(unset) badge logic, title-case default, popover toggle, local override surfacing, empty-providers placeholder, env-var pattern attribute), significanceFlaggingSection.test.ts (6 tests: command name + annotation syntax + queue path, button binding, honor checkbox default ON, explicit OFF, indicators), notificationsSection.test.ts (7 tests: three controls, disabled test button + Session-7 label, default env-var names, env-var badge logic, indicators, pattern attribute), localOverridesSummarySection.test.ts (6 tests: absent file, empty, populated diff listing, local-only section marker, open button, scalar formatting), patch.test.ts (24 tests: every field route + no-op detection + demote from local to shared + content hash + comment preservation), configEditor-e2e.test.ts (3 tests: all sections render against realistic state; save round-trip across all sections; hand-edit invalid value surfaces in next-load validation), halfBatchRecovery.test.ts (8 tests: content-hash drift detection + classify save attempts). Final unit suite: 38 -> 109 tests passing; tsc clean. Note: ConfigEditorPanel-foundation.test.ts continues to run only inside electron (imports vscode) — unchanged from Session 4.

configEditor-e2e.test.ts exercises the full pipeline: load three YAMLs, derive SectionState, render all six sections, apply a multi-section SavePayload via applyPatch, validate the resulting batch via validateBatch, write each Document via writeYamlFile, reload, re-derive state, and assert that each section's HTML reflects the saved values. Plus hand-edit-introduces-invalid-value scenario to confirm next-load validation surfaces drift. All 3 e2e tests pass. The 'Run flag command' and 'Send test notification' surfaces ship in later sessions per spec; Session 5 surfaces just the buttons with graceful fallbacks.

Cross-provider verification via gpt-5-4. Session 5's surface (12 files, ~2011 LOC) exceeded the single-call read-timeout for high-effort reasoning; split into three sub-rounds: Part A (8 section files + helpers + types, ~720 LOC), Part B1 (patch.ts alone, ~304 LOC), Part B2 (ConfigEditorPanel.ts alone, ~977 LOC). Findings + fixes: (A) 1 Minor — §2 Budget cost-messaging copy missing multi-week scale element from feedback_user_facing_cost_messaging memory; fixed by adding '$0-$200/month' + '$5-$50 per 2-3 week set' framing alongside existing $0-~$50/week range. Test budgetSection.test.ts strengthened to assert all four required elements. (B1) 2 Major — (i) applyPatch over-reported PatchApplyResult.*Changed flags on no-op saves (always set true regardless of whether value actually changed); fixed by introducing setIfChanged(doc, path, value) helper that compares doc.getIn(path) === value before mutating, with flag-set only on actual mutation. (ii) threshold_usd and warn_at_percent shared branches didn't demote — an existing local override would still win after the save; fixed by adding deleteIfPresent(localOverridesDoc, ...) calls in the shared branches. Tests added: no-op-save, partial-no-op, threshold/warn/outsourcing demote-from-local. (B2 round 1) 2 Blocker + 2 Major + 1 Minor — (i) Blocker: _detectDrift overwrote active failed-write _recovery; fixed by guarding 'if (!this._recovery || this._recovery.failed.length === 0)' around drift detection. (ii) Blocker: _lastSaveSnapshot used doc.toString() / null inconsistently; refactored to use raw text comparison (SaveSnapshot.routerConfigText/budgetText/localOverridesText) and new _captureSnapshot helper. (iii) Major: retry didn't refresh baseline on full success; fixed by calling _captureSnapshot in _retryFailedWrite. (iv) Major: _reapplyLastSave rewrote disk-loaded state, not last save; fixed by writing _lastSaveSnapshot.*Text bytes directly. (v) Minor: truncation-to-empty drift hidden by truthiness checks; fixed by replacing with explicit !== null. (B2 round 2) 1 Blocker + 2 Major — (i) Blocker: half-batch retry sourced docs from _loaded.*Doc after _refresh repopulated them from disk (so retry would write wrong bytes, or fail when localOverridesDoc==null after refresh); fixed by adding HalfBatchRecovery.pendingContents Map<file,string> populated in _handleSave BEFORE _refresh, and _retryFailedWrite writes from the cache. (ii) Major: _handleSave passed _loaded.*Doc directly into applyPatch — a failed validation left in-memory state mutated without rollback; fixed by cloning via parseDocumentFromText(doc.toString()) and only committing clones to _loaded.*Doc after validation passes. (iii) Major: _reapplyLastSave wrote three files inside one try block, no per-file recovery; fixed by adopting the same per-file succeeded/failed pattern + setting _recovery on partial failure. (B2 round 3) Rate-limited 429 from OpenAI after 3 retries; deferred re-confirmation — operator can re-run scripts/verify_session_026_5_b2.py once the TPM bucket clears. All findings from B2 rounds 1 and 2 are addressed in code per the verifier's specific guidance; unit tests cover the new behaviors. Total verification spend Session 5: $1.218 (cumulative Set 026 NTE: $3.07 of $10.00).

Created tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/configEditor/annotationParser.ts: pure regex parser for `# @dabbler:outsource-review("...")` (Python/shell/YAML) and `// @dabbler:outsource-review("...")` (JS/TS/Java/C#/C/C++/Go) annotations. findAnnotations(text, filePath, now?) returns {ts, reason, source: 'annotation', file, line} per match — non-greedy reason capture stopping at the first unescaped `"`; escaped quotes/backslashes unescaped; raw `\r`/`\n` forbidden inside reason (verifier-driven hardening — see Step 7); empty reasons rejected; line-number computation via binary search over precomputed newline offsets; file path POSIX-normalized via replace(/\\/g, '/'). deduplicateAnnotations(incoming, existing) keyed on file+line+reason.

Created ai_router/decision_review_queue.py: read_queue(session_set_dir) -> list[dict], clear_queue(session_set_dir) -> int, queue_path(session_set_dir) -> Path. Tolerates missing/empty file (returns []), malformed lines (skip with WARN), non-object lines (skip with WARN). clear_queue is idempotent (multiple calls return 0 after the first). DECISION_REVIEW_QUEUE_FILENAME exported alongside. Re-exported from ai_router/__init__.py as read_decision_review_queue / clear_decision_review_queue / decision_review_queue_path (aliased to disambiguate from session_events.read_events).

Created tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/commands/decisionReviewQueue.ts (pure helpers: appendQueueEntry, findActiveSessionSetDir, QueueEntry type, QUEUE_FILENAME constant) and tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/commands/flagDecisionForReview.ts (vscode wiring: input box -> JSONL append with source: 'command', file: null, line: null; cancel/empty-reason silent no-op; missing-active-set info notification; error-on-append surfaces as error notification rather than propagating). Split into pure + wiring so the helpers can be unit-tested via plain mocha + ts-node without the @vscode/test-electron harness.

Created tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/commands/annotationScanner.ts (pure helpers: scanFilesForAnnotations with FileReader test seam, loadHonorAnnotationsToggle reading ai_router/local-overrides.yaml -> decision_review.honor_annotations defaulting true, loadExistingQueueEntries deserializing the queue into dedup keys, toPosixPath helper, SCAN_GLOB covering ts/tsx/js/jsx/mjs/cjs/py/rb/go/rs/java/cs/kt/swift/c/cc/cpp/h/hpp/sh/bash/zsh/ps1/yaml/yml/toml, SCAN_EXCLUDE_GLOB for node_modules/dist/out/build/.venv/venv/__pycache__/.git) and tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/commands/scanAnnotationsForActiveSet.ts (vscode wiring: workspace.findFiles -> scan -> dedup against existing queue -> append fresh entries). Wiring uses vscode.RelativePattern so the glob is scoped to the active set's workspace root rather than the session-set dir.

Created tests: src/test/suite/annotationParser.test.ts (21 tests: # and // comment styles, no-marker rejection, multi-match order, CR/LF + first-line line numbers, escape handling, unicode round-trip, empty-reason rejection, nested parens, newline-rejection in reason, path normalization both styles + mixed, dedup keying, ts/source ignored); src/test/suite/flagDecisionForReview.test.ts (9 tests: appendQueueEntry create+append+unicode; findActiveSessionSetDir null/single/multi-tie-break/null-lastTouched; writer is dumb-append-only; dedup is reader-side); src/test/suite/scanAnnotationsForActiveSet.test.ts (26 tests: scan empty/multi-file/relative/host-independent-POSIX/read-error-skip/multi-annotation; toggle default-true + explicit-false + non-boolean fallback + lookup path; queue loader absent/dedup-key-shape/malformed-skip/injected-reader; toPosixPath unit; SCAN_GLOB content; end-to-end dedup re-scan no-op + new-annotation appends; cross-host backslash queue replay); src/test/suite/significanceFlaggingSection.test.ts strengthened (8 tests: command name + annotation syntax + queue path; button id; honor default ON + explicit OFF; indicators; describes workspace-scan command not session-start auto-scan; documents both # and // styles). ai_router/tests/test_decision_review_queue_reader.py (13 tests: read empty/file-absent/multi-entry-order/blank-line-skip/malformed-skip-with-WARN/non-object-skip-with-WARN/extra-fields-preserved/unicode; clear absent-returns-zero/removes-and-counts/idempotent/counts-only-parseable; queue_path filename). tsc clean.

Wired commands into the extension surface. package.json contributes.commands: added 'dabbler.flagDecisionForReview' ('Flag Decision for Cross-Provider Review') and 'dabbler.scanAnnotationsForActiveSet' ('Scan Workspace for @dabbler:outsource-review Annotations'), both under category 'Dabbler'. extension.ts: imported registerFlagDecisionForReview + registerScanAnnotationsForActiveSet; added two safeRegister calls alongside the existing config-editor registration. ConfigEditorPanel.ts._runFlagDecisionCommand: removed the Session-5 graceful-fallback branch — the command is registered alongside the panel from this version on, so executeCommand runs unconditionally. significanceFlaggingSection.ts: docblock updated to describe the now-shipping commands; rendered HTML copy corrected (verifier-driven, Step 7) to describe the actual workspace-walk command rather than the never-implemented 'orchestrator scans open files at session start' promise.

Added 'Significance flagging' section to docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md under Key Concepts, between 'Cross-Provider Verification' and 'Session-Set Lifecycle and State File'. Documents both surfaces (command + annotation), the queue file path, the JSON line shape for each source, the honor-annotations toggle and its default, and the orchestrator-side read+clear flow that consumes the queue at session start. Updated ai_router/CHANGELOG.md [0.3.0] with a Session-6 'Added' block citing decision_review_queue.py + DECISION_REVIEW_QUEUE_FILENAME. Updated tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/CHANGELOG.md [0.13.15] with a Session-6 'Added' block citing both commands + the annotation parser + the pure-helper split + the section copy correction.

Cross-provider verification via gpt-5-4 across two slices. First attempt 429'd with OpenAI insufficient_quota (not rate-limit); operator topped up. Slice A (Python queue reader + annotation parser + section comment + workflow-doc paragraph, ~330 LOC production): 3 Major findings. (1) ANNOTATION_RE allowed CR/LF inside the reason capture so an unterminated `"` could stitch to a later line's `")`; tightened to [^"\\\\\\r\\n] + 3 tests for newline/CRLF/unterminated-then-later-line. (2) Path normalization used path.sep-based split (host-dependent); rewrote to replace(/\\\\/g, '/') so Windows-style input normalizes on POSIX hosts too + tests for mixed/already-POSIX. (3) Section HTML said 'orchestrator scans open files at session start; any new annotations are queued automatically' — stale Session-5 copy promising never-shipped behavior; rewrote to name the workspace-scan command + dedup behavior + both comment styles, with 2 strengthened rendering tests. Slice B (TS commands + extension wiring, ~390 LOC production): 1 Major + 1 Minor. (4) Operator-layer normalization missing — scanFilesForAnnotations and loadExistingQueueEntries took host-native paths through to dedup; added toPosixPath helper + applied at both call sites + cross-host queue-replay test confirming Windows-written queue entries collide with POSIX re-scan. (5) Existing path test was host-conditional (constructed input with path.sep so the bug couldn't surface off Windows); added a literal-Windows-input test that asserts backslash-free output regardless of host. Final test counts: 120 pure-unit TS (+18 annotation, +9 flag, +26 scan, +2 significance, plus existing 65); 427 Python (+13 queue reader, unchanged otherwise). tsc clean. Verification spend Session 6: $0.426. Cumulative Set 026 NTE: $3.50 of $10.00.
Started Session 7 (final session of Set 026). session-state.json flipped currentSession 6 -> 7. Budget position: $3.50 of $10.00 NTE spent across Sessions 1-6.

Test-notification button wired. notificationsSection.ts: removed disabled attribute and (wired in Session 7) placeholder. Added s5 button click handler in _getHtml() script.

Documentation updates. docs/quick-start.md: new Configuring your project (Full tier) section with six-section table and shared/local field split explanation. docs/adoption-bootstrap.md: Step 9 closing pointers now include Configuring the AI router visually subsection pointing at Dabbler: Open Dabbler Config Editor. CLAUDE.md: version line bumped to v0.13.15; new Router-config editor subsection documenting the editor file layout and key files.

CHANGELOG.md (extension) expanded. Added Session 7 Added block to [0.13.15] section covering wizard integration, test-notification button wiring, doc updates (quick-start, adoption-bootstrap, CLAUDE.md).
Build checks: tsc --noEmit clean; pytest 427 passed, 1 skipped. No regressions.

Cross-provider verification via gpt-5-4 (single slice, all Session 7 production changes). 1 Blocker + 2 Minors found. Blocker: wizard.html inline onclick attributes violate nonce-based CSP; all six action buttons converted to data-command attributes with addEventListener binding in the nonce-bearing script block. Minor 1: missing openExternal case in WizardPanel.ts (pricing-link click posted openExternal but panel never handled it); added case with URL validation. Minor 2: duplicate error toast on spawn failure (child.on error + child.on close both showed errors); added spawnErrored flag, close handler returns early if error fired. All three fixed. Verification spend: $0.358. Cumulative Set 026 NTE: $3.50 + $0.36 = $3.86 of $10.00.
